dependencies: Significant portion of engines purchased from Cummins Inc.
Significant portion of transmissions from Eaton Corporation Plc and ZF Friedrichshafen AG
Significant portion of North America stampings from Magna International Inc.
Semiconductors as essential component sourced from various suppliers
DAF purchases fully assembled cabs from Renault V.I. for European light/medium-duty product line
risks: Loss of supply from Cummins, Eaton, ZF or Magna would have material effect on results
Sudden inability of supplier to deliver components could have temporary adverse effect
Suppliers also supply automotive manufacturers - automotive industry issues can affect PACCAR suppliers
Increased demand exceeding supplier production capacity

regulatory_risks: National Traffic and Motor Vehicle Safety Act and Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards
Environmental laws and regulations in all operating countries
EPA Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards and Fuel Efficiency Standards for Medium and Heavy-Duty Engines and Vehicles
European Union CO2 emission reduction targets and regulations
Regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council on Monitoring and Reporting of CO2 Emissions from New Heavy-Duty Vehicles
California Air Resources Board Heavy-Duty Omnibus Regulation
California Advanced Clean Truck (ACT) regulation requiring increasing percentage of zero emissions trucks
ACT regulation adopted by several other states
International trade regulations including customs and import/export laws
Government embargoes and sanctions prohibiting sales to specific persons or countries
Privacy laws and government regulations on telematics data
Cellular frequency allocations regulated by government agencies
Changes in statutory income tax rates
Extensive trade, competition and anti-corruption laws in global operations

sanction_risks: Government embargoes and sanctions prohibiting sales to specific persons or countries
International trade policy changes may impact demand and competitive position
